videos.title: What gear ratio should you run? #jeep #gears #overlanding #4x4
5 Things That Could Save Your Jeep. And Maybe You.
videos.description:
You can build the most capable Jeep on the planet, but if you head out on an adventure without the right gear inside the cab, none of it matters when things go sideways. And things do go sideways.

#JeepLife #JeepGear #Overlanding #OffRoad 

In this episode Paul and The Ginger walk through the five things that should be inside every Jeep before you leave the driveway. 
Not mods. Not suspension. Not tires. 
The stuff you pack in the cab, doors closed, that could save your trip, your rig, and poss

videos.description:
AI can generate more words faster than ever, but are those words actually creating value? In this video, you’ll learn the difference between AI Slop, AI Dribble, and AI Boost.
And how to make sure tools like Microsoft Copilot improve your work instead of making it longer, noisier, or less accurate.

You’ll see what happens when three useful bullet points become a giant “professional” email, only for the recipient to ask Copilot to turn it back into bullet points. You’ll also see why a perfectly

videos.title: Yes, AI agents hallucinate. Here's how mine caught itself. Get the multi-agent system below ⬇️
videos.description:
Everyone's first objection to AI agents is the same, and it is a fair one. They hallucinate. You cannot trust them. So you end up hovering over every step, checking the work, and at that point you may as well have done it yourself.

Here is the thing that changes the math. The fix for an agent you cannot trust is not a smarter single agent. It is a second agent whose only job is to check the first one, and a third that checks that. When one of mine made something up, another one caught it and co

videos.title: Stop building AI agents that just click buttons #AI #aiagents #automation #productivity #AItools
videos.description:
Most agent demos show you the wrong magic trick. Watch, it clicked send. Watch, it booked the meeting. That is the easy part. You could always click send. What actually eats your week is everything that happens before the click: digging through a messy folder, making sense of a denial letter, pulling the right numbers out of a pile of unstructured junk that was never meant to be read quickly.

That is the part worth automating. An agent earns its place when it takes the bureaucratic mess and tur

videos.title: China's K3 Model Reveals the Problem With Open Weights
videos.description:
Full post:
https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/p/kimi-k3-open-weights-cost?r=1z4sm5&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&showWelcomeOnShare=true

Kimi K3 is Moonshot's new open-weight AI model, and it complicates the story that Chinese open models are cheap, efficient, and closing the gap on the frontier. Here's what it actually costs to run, and what it means for OpenAI, Anthropic, and the future of open source.

My Links 🔗
👉🏻 Newsletter: https://natesnewsletter.substack.com/

videos.title: Claude Cowork Just Went Fully Hands-Off (Scheduled Tasks V2)
videos.description: Tired of complex automation setups? Claude Code's new scheduled tasks feature lets you automate repeating business processes directly in the cloud, no server or technical skills required. In this video, I'll show you how this game-changing update works by walking through real-world accounting examples and building a subscription audit task from scratch. Learn how to set up your own automations that run even when your laptop is shut.

videos.title: Is Kimi K3 Really Fable Class
videos.description:
Moonshot’s Kimi K3 is the strongest open-weight model yet, with benchmarks approaching Fable 5 and GPT-5.6. But early testing reveals major limitations in reliability, speed, and cost. NLW examines whether K3 lives up to the hype—and what it means for open models, AI safety, and the US-China race.

The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention - Data Security for non-Microsoft connected apps
items.description: Microsoft Purview Data Loss Prevention and Information Protection Auto-labeling capabilities now extend to non-Microsoft connected apps (e.g., Google Workspace, Box, Dropbox, Salesforce, ServiceNow, Amazon Web Services, and Cisco Webex). The conditions and actions supported for each of these applications vary.

items.title: PowerPoint: Create on brand presentations with additional brand assets
items.description: Seamlessly access your brand colors, images, icons, logos and more right within PowerPoint to create on-brand presentations.

items.title: OneDrive: Document library views now available in OneDrive
items.description: Users can now work with SharePoint document library views directly from OneDrive. In addition to switching between existing views, users can create and manage views to customize how library content is organized and displayed. This helps teams surface the information that matters most while staying within the OneDrive experience.

items.title: OneDrive: New Shortcuts folder option when adding shortcuts
items.description: OneDrive makes it easy to add shortcuts to the files and folders you use most. Now you can choose to keep those shortcuts neatly together in one place instead of mixed in with everything else. When you add a shortcut, you can send it to a dedicated "Shortcuts" folder rather than your OneDrive root. The folder is created automatically the first time you use it and is easy to spot, with a distinct color and building icon. The Shortcuts folder behaves like any other folder—move it, rename it, share

items.title: Microsoft 365 admin center: Organizational Data – Granular access policy controls for custom attributes
items.description: We are introducing enhanced access policy management for Organizational Data in Microsoft 365. This update enables admins to release custom attributes to specific users or groups, rather than broadly to all employees or all managers. This provides a more conservative default approach for managing sensitive organizational data, while also supporting staged rollout of new attributes. Additionally, admins can configure whether leaders and managers are permitted to share non-public data with Workfor

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Proximity join support for presenters in Teams events from Teams Rooms on Windows and Android
items.description: Presenters can take advantage of proximity join with Teams Rooms on Windows or Android for fast, effortless connection to nearby room systems during Teams events, such as town halls, webinars, and structured meetings. This feature enables seamless live presentations.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Meeting toolbar redesigned
items.description: You can now customize the meeting toolbar by pinning, unpinning, and reordering controls to match how you work. Raise Hand is grouped under Reactions to reduce mis-clicks, and Leave is clearly separated on the right. It may feel different at first, but it’s designed to be faster and easier to use.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Entra passwordless resource account support for Teams Rooms on Windows devices
items.description: Teams rooms on Windows supports Entra Resource Accounts for secure, passwordless sign-in that separates device and user identities. IT admins can migrate to Entra accounts easily with the migration wizard, and track progress using a dashboard in the Pro Management portal.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Improved organization for muted and meeting chats
items.description: Microsoft Teams automatically organizes your chats with new built in sections that help reduce clutter and keep conversations easy to find. Muted chats (on by default) are grouped together in a dedicated area, while meeting chats (off by default) can be collected into a single section for quick access. You’re always in control, as each section can be turned on or off based on your preferences. The new Meeting chats section will replace the Meeting chats filter, that was previously available at t

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Security Investigations – analyze files tied to endpoint DLP alerts
items.description: Speed up analysis of exfiltrated content by launching Data Security Investigations (DSI) from endpoint Data Loss Prevention (DLP) alerts. In DSI, define your endpoint DLP query (for example, time range, users, and endpoints). DSI then automatically gathers the related files that triggered the alerts for review (for example, UserA downloaded a file on 3/1/2026).

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention – File Quarantine Action in DLP for SharePoint/OneDrive
items.description: File Quarantine action in Microsoft Purview DLP for SharePoint and OneDrive, enables stronger, immediate protection for sensitive data. When a DLP policy is triggered, the File Quarantine action automatically moves the file to a restricted, admin‑controlled & defined quarantine location—instantly removing access for all users while preserving the file for review and investigation. This capability helps organizations contain data‑exposure risks, prevents further sharing or misuse, and delivers a

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Camera view switching in multi-camera Teams Rooms on Windows
items.description: When in a meeting with another Teams Rooms on Windows that has multiple cameras, you can switch camera views in that room via the participant panel on the console, or video tile on a touch board, to optimize your views.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Branded Meeting Reactions
items.description: With new branded reactions, organizations can now extend their visual identity directly into meetings. IT admins simply upload custom reaction icons reflecting brand elements or event themes, and these instantly become available for meeting participants.  A simple way to create more cohesive, on-brand meeting experiences.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): [Copilot Search] [Copilot Search] Copilot Chat available when using Copilot Search
items.description: Copilot Chat will be available for Microsoft 365 Copilot users from the side pane on the right-hand side when using Copilot Search in the Microsoft 365 Copilot app. This allows users to engage with Copilot while browsing universal search without switching pages or losing context.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Seamless Search and Chat Integration
items.description: Microsoft 365 Chat brings the power of conversational AI directly into Microsoft 365 Copilot Search, enabling users to move effortlessly from finding information to acting on it. Search results become the foundation for contextual chat, allowing you to ask follow-up questions, synthesize insights, and generate content—all in one unified experience. This integration reduces workflow fragmentation, accelerates decision-making, and delivers a more intuitive way to interact with your organization’s

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Viva Engage - Agents in communities
items.description: Agents in Viva Engage communities supports communities by answering questions, facilitating knowledge sharing, citing sources, and scaling expertise.

items.title: Viva Glint - Action Taking Analysis Report
items.description: Introduction of a new report that provides transparency into platform activity by dashboard users. Metrics such as report views, Team Conversations completed, Focus Areas created, and more will be visible and filterable with this report.

items.title: Microsoft Viva: Introducing the New Republish Feature in Viva Amplify
items.description: We’re excited to launch Republish, a new feature that keeps your content fresh, extends its reach, and maximizes engagement across SharePoint, Viva Engage, Outlook, and Teams. Update existing published content in a few clicks, re-share key messages to new audiences or channels, correct errors and refine your messaging —all without starting from scratch. Republish in Viva Amplify makes content management effortless.

items.title: OneNote: Multimodal capture in Copilot Notebooks (Android)
items.description: Multimodal capture enables users to transcribe audio, take images, and type notes in a single session. Copilot then creates a structured Copilot Page with insights and user-captured content, saved to a user-selected Copilot Notebook. Designed for capturing offline work moments—including in-person conversations, whiteboard sessions, and personal notes. This capability is already generally available on OneNote for iPhone and iPad.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot Studio: Share Autonomous Agents to End Users (Run-Only)
items.description: Previously, autonomous agents could only be used and managed by makers, limiting their reach to the people who needed them. Run-only sharing enables makers to securely share autonomous agents with end users, allowing users to benefit from agent-driven automation without requiring edit permissions or ownership. This extends autonomous agent capabilities to a broader audience while maintaining governance and control.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Power BI integration in M365 Copilot
items.description: This release enables M365 Copilot to reason over Power BI enterprise data and returns grounded answers directly from Power BI reports and semantic models. Users can ask natural‑language questions and receive accurate answers.

items.title: SharePoint: Flexible Section Authoring Improvements
items.description: We've enhanced Flexible Sections in SharePoint Pages to make page layout creation more intuitive and predictable. Authors can now use visual column layout guides that mirror familiar sections, keep gridlines and layout guides visible while editing, and convert existing sections into Flexible Sections with a single click while preserving content placement. These improvements help authors create richer page designs with greater confidence and consistency across desktop, mobile, and email experienc

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Unified Agent and App Installation Management Across Microsoft 365 and Teams Admin Center
items.description: Administrators using the Microsoft 365 admin center and Teams admin center will be able to apply app and agent installation changes once and have them consistently enforced across Teams, Outlook, and Microsoft 365 Copilot. Previously, installation changes made in the Microsoft 365 admin center only applied to Outlook and Microsoft 365, while changes made in the Teams admin center only applied to Teams. With this update, app and agent installation is unified across all supported surfaces, enablin

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Teams events attendance and engagement report policy
items.description: Teams events will soon have a separate attendance and engagement report policy from the "CsTeamsMeetingPolicy" attendance and engagement report policy. This will permit admins to allow Teams events organizers to access attendance and engagement reports, while restricting access to Teams meeting attendance and engagement reports.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Multi-line Calling on Teams Mobile
items.description: Make and receive calls using multiple assigned phone numbers directly from your mobile device - choose the right line for every call, with unified call history and seamless switching across all your lines.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Declarative agents with Actions available in GCC High and DoD
items.description: Declarative Agents with Actions enable Microsoft 365 Copilot to take real-world actions by invoking APIs, plugins, and enterprise workflows directly within the chat experience. Using custom actions, declarative agents can call external systems, execute operations, and retrieve or update data, moving beyond Q&A to full task completion. These actions are powered through API plugins, MCP tools, and enterprise connectors, allowing seamless integration with line-of-business applications and services.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Shared & Delegate Mailbox Scheduling for Events
items.description: Empower Chiefs of Staff and delegated roles to efficiently organize events on behalf of leaders or teams using shared or delegated mailboxes. By sending trusted invites from the chosen mailbox, this capability ensures consistent identity, improved trust, and smoother event setup.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Save your event as template to reuse event configurations
items.description: Organizers for Teams events instances will now be able to reuse their event configurations in the Events app in Teams. Organizers can save these configurations as a template in the Events app to use again for future Teams events.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention to restrict processing external emails in Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Chat
items.description: We are expanding Microsoft Purview DLP for Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Chat to safeguard risks from external emails. This real-time control helps organizations mitigate data risks by preventing Microsoft 365 Copilot and Chat from processing emails from senders external to your organization.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Room availability signal for Teams events
items.description: In the Events app in Teams, Teams events organizers will now be able to see if the chosen room or space they have selected for their event is available at the designated time or not. This feature is available for any Teams event organizer with a Teams Enterprise license.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Lifecycle Management – Archive OneDrive and SharePoint files under retention
items.description: Retention based file archiving moves inactive content to low-cost storage, lowering costs without compromising on Compliance - while keeping data discoverable and Copilot results highly relevant.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Loss Prevention – Enrich Defender alerts Graph API with DLP event data
items.description: Enhance current API infrastructure to provide easy and simple way for customers to export data to integrate with SIEM tools, create automated workflows and generate customizable reports. Today alert data is present in Graph API and DLP rule match event details are present in Management API. This work enriches the graph API with DLP event data to make correlation and integration easy for customers.

items.title: Microsoft Purview: Insider Risk Management- Data Security Triage Agent in IRM Enhancements
items.description:
The Data Security Triage Agent in Insider Risk Management is deploying enhancements such as improved user risk and activity explorer pattern summaries to support improved investigation accuracy, context, and decision quality. 

Microsoft Purview Insider Risk Management correlates various signals to identify potential malicious or inadvertent insider risks, such as IP theft, data leakage, and security violations. Insider Risk Management enables customers to create policies to manage security and

items.title: SharePoint: Admin policy for expiring "People in your organization" links
items.description: Administrators can now set organization-wide policies to automatically expire "People in your organization" sharing links after a specified period. This helps organizations maintain tighter control over internal content sharing by ensuring links don't remain active indefinitely. Configure expiration timeframes that align with your organization's data governance requirements—whether that's 30 days, 90 days, or a custom period.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Business Justification for Copilot License Requests
items.description: When requesting a Microsoft 365 Copilot license, users can now provide a business justification explaining why they need Copilot. This context is shown to admins during review, helping them make faster, more informed approval decisions and supporting governance and audit needs. Updated July 20, 2026: We have decided to rollback this change and will not be moving forward with it at this time. We apologize for any inconvenience.

items.title: Microsoft Teams: Teams frontline BYOD onboarding wizard
items.description: Simplify frontline onboarding with an improved Teams mobile experience on personal devices. Workers can easily download and start using the Teams mobile app on their own device in compliance with organizational security policies, reducing the need for manager assistance and IT support. Updated July 20, 2026: We have decided not to move forward with this change at this time. We apologize for any inconvenience.

items.title: Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Domain exclusion for web grounding
items.description: This feature will allow admins to specify a limited set of sites to exclude from web grounding in M365 Copilot and Copilot Chat #copilotcontrolsystem

items.title: OneNote: Summary and Q&A in OneNote Mobile with Copilot (Iphone)
items.description: With this new Summary feature in OneNote mobile, you can create a short and fluent summary of your longer pages which will help to consume relevant information faster. With the Question and Answer at your fingertips, you can get real-time answers to your queries directly within your notes, enhancing your ability to access and utilize information quickly and effectively. We inadvertently marked this item as rolling out and now have updated it to be accurate.  We apologize for any inconvenience.

items.title: OneNote: Summary and Q&A in OneNote Mobile with Copilot (Android)
items.description: With this new Summary feature in OneNote mobile, you can create a short and fluent summary of your longer pages which will help to consume relevant information faster. With the Question and Answer at your fingertips, you can get real-time answers to your queries directly within your notes, enhancing your ability to access and utilize information quickly and effectively.
